iterations.theme: The '40 Days for Life' Initiative: Personal Testimonies, Societal Impact, and Call to Action Against Abortion in Croatia
iterations.reason_for_cut: The provided transcript is shorter than the requested minimum iteration length (35-45 minutes), so the entire content is presented as a single thematic unit covering the full podcast.

chapters.topic: Introduction to Ante Čajkušić and '40 Days for Life' initiative
Launch of the Lenten prayer campaign and mothers' regrets
Personal stories of women affected by abortion (forced abortion, multiple abortions)
Testimony of a father whose wife aborted behind his back
Vision for a joyful Croatia filled with children
Impact of the initiative: children saved and growth of 'Centers for Life'
Healing from abortion trauma and the role of volunteers
Disturbing testimony of fetal remains in hospital equipment
Call for a Croatia that values every child's life
Abortion as a threat to the dignity of mothers, fathers, and the nation
The inherent value and uniqueness of every human life
Focus on eugenic abortions and misdiagnoses
Importance of seeking multiple medical opinions (Father Marinko's story)
Stories of perseverance: Father Slavko and his daughter
The miraculous survival of Petar Šimun (Father Ivan's son)
The crucial role of fathers in preventing abortions
Details of the 40-day campaign and call for public evangelization
Historical context of public prayer in Croatia
Testimonies of lives changed and children saved through the vigil
Appeal to bishops and the need for more workers in the movement
Achievements of 'Hrvatska za život' (Walk for Life, aid to pregnant women)
Final call to action: stopping abortion for Croatia's future
Concluding remarks and blessing
